$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to artisanpack namespace; attributes/supports/styles verbatim (inner-block + summary attribute schema preserved).

TypeScript port. Adds explicit 'wp-block-details' class to useBlockProps so the editor canvas matches frontend independent of __experimentalSelector internals. Drops the private withIgnoreIMEEvents wrapper from componentsPrivateApis.
TypeScript port; class list adds 'wp-block-details' explicitly (parity with edit). Preserves InnerBlocks.Content rendering.
Upstream ships no deprecated.js; fork exports an empty array from index.ts to preserve the standard contract.

Upstream multi-block conversion preserved (targets the new namespace via metadata.name); added bidirectional block transforms for core/details ↔ artisanpack/details round-tripping including inner blocks.
Combined SCSS-to-CSS port. No data-type selectors in upstream, but namespace would map to artisanpack/details if added.
Inline SVG mirroring @wordpress/icons' details icon (editor doesn't bundle dashicons).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract. Upstream's PHP-side init wiring (index.php) and example settings are not carried.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx + save.tsx add 'wp-block-details' to useBlockProps explicitly; upstream relies on __experimentalSelector to inject it.

edit.tsx drops the private withIgnoreIMEEvents wrapper from componentsPrivateApis; key handlers run unconditionally. Acceptable since IME composition events do not fire 'Enter' on commit in modern browsers.
edit.tsx omits useToolsPanelDropdownMenuProps (a private hook from `../utils/hooks`) — the ToolsPanel renders without the dropdown menu props.
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that don't exist upstream — required to coexist with core/details during the V2 rollout. Inner blocks are cloned in both directions.
index.ts replaces upstream's PHP-side init wiring (index.php) with the visual-editor auto-discovery contract; the `example` settings object isn't carried.
Frontend rendering delegates to the renderer-blade core/details template via the artisanpack/details.blade.php shim — upstream relies on a dynamic block (index.php) which is unused here.
